tools.name: create_alert
tools.description:
Create a campsite availability alert that monitors for openings and notifies the user.

Requires an Outdoorithm API key (generate at outdoorithm.com/dashboard/api-keys).
The alert runs continuously, checking every 2-15 minutes depending on subscription tier.

Args:
    api_key: User's Outdoorithm AP

tools.name: list_alerts
tools.description:
List all campsite availability alerts for an Outdoorithm user.

Requires an Outdoorithm API key (generate at outdoorithm.com/dashboard/api-keys).

Args:
    api_key: User's Outdoorithm API key from their dashboard settings.
    status: Filter by alert status. One of: "active", "paused", "expired",

tools.name: toggle_alert
tools.description:
Pause or resume a campsite availability alert.

If the alert is active, it will be paused (stops checking for availability).
If the alert is paused, it will be resumed (starts checking again).

Requires an Outdoorithm API key (generate at outdoorithm.com/dashboard/api-keys).

Args:
    api_key: User

tools.name: delete_alert
tools.description:
Permanently delete a campsite availability alert.

This cannot be undone. All associated notification history will also be deleted.
Consider using toggle_alert to pause instead of deleting.

Requires an Outdoorithm API key (generate at outdoorithm.com/dashboard/api-keys).

Args:
    api_key: User's

tools.name: build_packing_list
tools.description:
Generate a context-aware packing checklist for a camping trip.

Returns categorized items from the gear catalog, adjusted for campground
amenities, activities, weather, and group size.

Args:
    campground_id: Optional CUID to personalize based on campground amenities
    trip_type: car_camping, ba

tools.name: calculate_drive_times
tools.description:
Calculate realistic drive times from an origin to multiple campgrounds using Mapbox routing with live traffic.

Uses the Mapbox Matrix API for traffic-aware routing. Maximum 9 destinations per request.

Args:
    origin_latitude: Latitude of starting location
    origin_longitude: Longitude of start

tools.name: check_availability
tools.description:
Check real-time campground availability for specific dates.

Queries the camply-service to check live campsite availability against
reservation systems. Supports 30+ providers including RecreationDotGov,
ReserveCalifornia, state parks, and county parks.

Args:
    campground_id: Campground CUID (e.g

tools.name: check_safety
tools.description:
Check campgrounds for safety issues, community atmosphere, and active alerts.

Checks:
- Greenbook community safety (vibe scores, discrimination reports, flag status)
- Active campground alerts (closures, restrictions) from enriched data
- Red flag detection (prevents recommending unsafe campgrounds

tools.name: compare_gear
tools.description:
Compare 2-3 gear items side-by-side with specs, pros/cons, verdicts, and comparison summary.

Supports lookup by unique_id with slug fallback. Use search_gear first if the
user hasn't named specific products.

Args:
    gear_ids: List of 2-3 gear item identifiers (unique_id or slug)

tools.name: find_similar_campgrounds
tools.description:
Find campgrounds similar to a given campground using vector embedding similarity.

Uses pre-computed embeddings combining review text (semantic) and structured attributes
(amenities, terrain, activities) for nuanced similarity matching.

Args:
    campground_id: CUID of the target campground to find

tools.name: get_campground_details
tools.description:
Get comprehensive details for a specific campground including amenities, reviews, safety, and seasonal ratings.

Returns ~80 curated fields (not the raw 214-column dump) including:
- Identity, location, fees, site info
- All amenity and activity booleans + descriptions
- Review summaries, best camps

tools.name: get_gear_for_campground
tools.description:
Get personalized gear recommendations for a specific campground.

Returns pre-computed recommendations across slot types: essential (must-have),
activity (activity-matched), comfort (nice-to-have), amenity_gap (compensates
for missing amenities). Each includes product details and purchase links.

Ar

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description:
Get camping-specific 7-day weather forecast with suitability ratings and gear recommendations.

Uses the Open-Meteo API (no key required) to provide:
- Daily high/low temps, conditions, precipitation, wind
- Camping suitability rating per day (excellent/good/fair/poor/not_recommended)
- Gear recomme

tools.name: prepare_reservation
tools.description:
Prepare a one-tap booking handoff for the user's chosen campground/dates.

Returns a pre-filled deep link to the operator's reservation page plus the
booking-window context (release date/time, ToS-compliant guidance, alert
suggestion) the agent needs to advise the user. Does NOT book on behalf —
thi

tools.name: search_campgrounds
tools.description:
Search for campgrounds with 40+ filters including location, amenities, activities, and community safety.

Requires at least one location constraint: near_location, latitude+longitude, or states.

Args:
    near_location: City, address, or landmark (e.g., "Oakland, CA", "Yosemite")
    latitude: Lati

tools.name: search_gear
tools.description:
Search the Outdoorithm gear catalog by keyword, category, price range, kit, necessity, or recommendation tier.

Returns matching products with prices, verdicts, and purchase links.
For campground-specific recommendations, see get_gear_for_campground.

Args:
    query: Text search across product name

tools.name: health_check
tools.description:
Check server health and connectivity.

Returns:
    Dictionary with health status including:
    - status: "healthy" or "unhealthy"
    - version: Server version
    - environment: Current environment (dev/staging/prod)

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "create_alert" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_campground_details" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Get comprehensive details for a specific campground including amenities, reviews, safety, and seasonal ratings. Returns ~80 curated fields (not the raw 214-colu…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "get_campground_details" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Get comprehensive details for a specific campground including amenities, reviews, safety, and seasonal ratings. Returns ~80 curated fields (not the raw 214-colu…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "search_campgrounds" is 2808 chars, far longer than this server's median (502). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Search for campgrounds with 40+ filters including location, amenities, activities, and community safety. Requires at least one location constraint: near_locatio…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: create_alert, delete_alert, check_safety, find_similar_campgrounds, get_campground_details, prepare_reservation, search_campgrounds · Private data: create_alert, list_alerts, toggle_alert, delete_alert, get_campground_details, search_campgrounds · Public sink: create_alert · Destructive: delete_alert, check_availability, get_campground_details, get_weather.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: create_alert, delete_alert, check_safety, find_similar_campgrounds, get_campground_details, prepare_reservation, search_campgrounds · Private data: create_alert, list_alerts, toggle_alert, delete_alert, get_campground_details, search_campgrounds · Public sink: create_alert · Destructive: delete_alert, check_availability, get_campground_details, get_weather

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
